seoTitle: YXC Crystal Resonators and Oscillators | Authorized Distributor | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Authorized distributor of YXC quartz crystals, crystal oscillators, automotive-grade timing devices, and programmable oscillators. Technical support, competitive pricing, fast delivery.

faqs.question: What is the difference between crystal resonator and crystal oscillator?
faqs.answer: A crystal resonator (passive crystal) requires external oscillator circuitry to generate a clock signal, while a crystal oscillator (active crystal) contains the complete oscillator circuit inside and outputs a ready-to-use clock signal. Oscillators are easier to use but cost more and consume more power.

faqs.question: How do I select the right load capacitance for my crystal?
faqs.answer: The load capacitance (CL) must match the crystal's specified value for accurate frequency. The total load capacitance is calculated as CL = (C1 × C2)/(C1 + C2) + Cstray, where Cstray is typically 3-5pF. Common values are 8pF, 10pF, 12pF, and 20pF.

faqs.question: What is AEC-Q200 qualification and why is it important for automotive?
faqs.answer: AEC-Q200 is the automotive industry's standard for passive component qualification. It includes rigorous testing for temperature cycling, mechanical shock, vibration, and other stresses. Automotive applications require AEC-Q200 qualified parts to ensure reliability over the vehicle's lifetime.

faqs.question: What frequency stability do I need for my application?
faqs.answer: Frequency stability requirements depend on your application: ±10-20ppm for general consumer electronics, ±20-30ppm for industrial applications, ±30-50ppm for automotive, and ±0.5-2.5ppm for precision timing or wireless communications.

categories.name: Crystal Resonators
categories.description: High-precision quartz crystal resonators in various packages from 1.6mm to 7.0mm, supporting frequencies from 8MHz to 100MHz with excellent frequency stability and low ESR.
categories.longDescription: YXC crystal resonators provide precise frequency control for microcontroller clocking, communication modules, and consumer electronics. As an authorized YXC distributor, BeiLuo offers comprehensive technical support, fast delivery, and competitive pricing. Our crystal resonator series includes popular packages like 3225, 2520, 2016, and 1612, with frequency stability options from ±10ppm to ±30ppm. Applications include IoT devices, industrial control systems, and portable electronics. Selection guides and application notes are available to help you choose the optimal crystal for your design.
categories.selectionGuide: Choose package size based on board space and handling requirements. 3225 is easiest to handle, 2016 is best for space-constrained designs. Select frequency stability based on application: ±10ppm for precision, ±20ppm for general use. Match load capacitance to your MCU's requirements.

faqs.question: What package size should I choose for my crystal?
faqs.answer: Choose 3.2×2.5mm (3225) for general applications with easier handling, 2.5×2.0mm (2520) for compact designs, or 2.0×1.6mm (2016) for ultra-compact wearables. Smaller packages have higher ESR and require more careful PCB layout.

faqs.question: How does load capacitance affect crystal frequency?
faqs.answer: Using a load capacitance different from the crystal's specification causes frequency error. Higher CL makes the crystal run slower, lower CL makes it run faster. The error is approximately 15-20ppm per pF of mismatch.

products.name: 25MHz Crystal Resonator 3.2×2.5mm
products.shortDescription: Compact 25MHz quartz crystal in 3.2×2.5mm package with ±10ppm stability. Ideal for microcontroller clocking and general purpose timing applications.
products.description: Compact 25MHz quartz crystal resonator in 3.2×2.5mm ceramic package, ideal for general purpose timing applications with excellent stability and reliability.
descriptionParagraphs: The YXC-3225-25M is a high-precision 25MHz quartz crystal resonator packaged in a compact 3.2×2.5mm ceramic SMD package. This popular package size offers an excellent balance between compact footprint and ease of handling during assembly.

With frequency stability of ±10ppm and low ESR of ≤60Ω, this crystal provides reliable clocking for microcontrollers, communication modules, and consumer electronics. The wide operating temperature range of -40°C to +85°C makes it suitable for industrial and commercial applications.

The 3225 package is widely supported by all major PCB assembly houses and is compatible with standard reflow soldering processes. This crystal is ideal for cost-sensitive designs where ±10ppm stability meets the application requirements.

pinout.description: 4-pin SMD package with 2 ground pins and 2 crystal connection pins

alternativeParts.description: Smaller 2.5×2.0mm package version with same electrical specs
alternativeParts.tradeOff: Smaller size but slightly higher ESR (~70Ω)
Larger PCB footprint but lower cost

companionParts.description: 25MHz active oscillator for plug-and-play operation without external caps
50MHz version for higher frequency microcontroller applications

faeReview.summary: This is an excellent general-purpose crystal with consistent quality across production lots. The 3225 package offers the best balance between compact size and ease of handling - it's large enough for reliable assembly while still fitting most space-constrained designs. I've recommended this part for dozens of microcontroller clocking applications and it has performed reliably. The ±10ppm stability is sufficient for most consumer and industrial applications. For cost-sensitive designs where you don't need the absolute tightest tolerance, this crystal delivers excellent value. Pay attention to load capacitance matching with your MCU's oscillator circuit for best frequency accuracy.
faeReview.author: Senior FAE Team

faqs.question: What load capacitors should I use with this crystal?
faqs.answer: For 12pF load capacitance, use two 22pF capacitors (assuming 3-4pF stray capacitance). The exact value depends on your PCB layout. Calculate using: CL = (C1 × C2)/(C1 + C2) + Cstray.

faqs.question: Can I use this crystal with 3.3V or 5V microcontrollers?
faqs.answer: Yes, the crystal itself is voltage-agnostic. The drive level from your MCU's oscillator circuit should not exceed 100μW. Most modern MCUs have configurable drive strength to accommodate different crystal specifications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between ±10ppm and ±20ppm stability?
faqs.answer: ±10ppm means the frequency can vary by ±250Hz at 25MHz, while ±20ppm allows ±500Hz variation. For UART communication at 115200 baud, ±10ppm ensures reliable communication without tuning, while ±20ppm may require baud rate calibration.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the ppm error from my target frequency?
faqs.answer: ppm = (Actual Frequency - Target Frequency) / Target Frequency × 1,000,000. For example, if your 25MHz crystal measures 25,000,250Hz, the error is +10ppm.

faqs.question: Can this crystal be used for USB applications?
faqs.answer: USB requires 48MHz with ±0.25% accuracy (2500ppm). While this crystal has much better stability (±10ppm), you would need a 48MHz crystal or use a PLL to generate 48MHz from 25MHz.

products.name: 32MHz Crystal Resonator 2.0×1.6mm
products.shortDescription: Ultra-compact 32MHz crystal in 2.0×1.6mm package for space-constrained wearable and IoT applications with ±10ppm stability.
products.description: Ultra-compact 32MHz crystal resonator in 2.0×1.6mm package, perfect for space-constrained applications like wearables and portable devices.
descriptionParagraphs: The YXC-2016-32M delivers 32MHz precision timing in an ultra-compact 2.0×1.6mm package, making it ideal for wearable devices, TWS earbuds, and other space-constrained applications where every square millimeter counts.

Despite its small size, this crystal maintains excellent electrical characteristics with ±10ppm frequency stability and low ESR of ≤80Ω. The optimized electrode design ensures reliable oscillation even with the reduced crystal mass in this miniature package.

The 0.45mm profile enables ultra-thin product designs. This crystal is particularly popular in Bluetooth LE applications where 32MHz is the standard frequency for Nordic, Silicon Labs, and other BLE SoCs.

companionParts.description: Active oscillator version for easier implementation

faeReview.summary: This is my go-to recommendation for wearable and portable applications where space is at a premium. The 2016 package is among the smallest available while still maintaining excellent electrical characteristics. I've used this in multiple TWS earbud designs and smartwatch projects with great success. The key consideration is PCB layout - the small pad size requires careful handling during assembly. Use a 4-mil stencil aperture and ensure your assembly house has experience with 0201-size components. For the crystal circuit, keep traces extremely short (<10mm) and use proper guard rings to prevent interference.
faeReview.author: Senior FAE Team

faqs.question: Is this crystal suitable for Bluetooth Low Energy applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, 32MHz is the standard frequency for many BLE SoCs including Nordic nRF52 series and Silicon Labs EFR32. The ±10ppm stability meets BLE requirements. Check your specific SoC's load capacitance requirements.

faqs.question: What are the assembly considerations for this small package?
faqs.answer: Use 4-mil stencil thickness with 1:1 aperture opening. Ensure your PCB has proper solder mask defined pads. The small size requires precise pick-and-place equipment. Avoid hand soldering - use reflow only.

faqs.question: Can I use this crystal with a standard 8-bit microcontroller?
faqs.answer: Yes, but verify your MCU can drive the 80Ω ESR. Some older 8-bit MCUs have weaker oscillator drivers designed for larger crystals with lower ESR. Check the MCU datasheet's crystal driver specifications.

faqs.question: How does the small package size affect shock resistance?
faqs.answer: The 2016 package actually has excellent shock resistance due to its low mass. YXC rates it for 5000g mechanical shock, same as larger packages. The ceramic package and optimized electrode design maintain reliability.

faqs.question: What is the typical current consumption with this crystal?
faqs.answer: Current depends on your MCU and load capacitors. Typical RTC-mode current is 1-3μA. For active clocking, expect 100-300μA depending on MCU and frequency. Use the lowest drive strength setting that ensures reliable oscillation.

categories.name: Crystal Oscillators
categories.description: High-performance crystal oscillators including programmable oscillators, TCXO, and differential output options for demanding timing applications.
categories.longDescription: YXC crystal oscillators provide precision timing with integrated oscillator circuits, eliminating the need for external load capacitors and simplifying design. As an authorized YXC distributor, BeiLuo offers a wide range of active oscillators including CMOS output for general applications, differential LVDS/LVPECL for high-speed communications, and programmable options for custom frequencies. Our oscillator portfolio covers 1MHz to 210MHz with stabilities from ±10ppm to ±100ppm. Applications include networking equipment, 5G base stations, industrial automation, and automotive systems. Technical support includes clock tree design assistance and jitter analysis.
categories.selectionGuide: Choose CMOS for general applications, differential for high-speed (>100MHz) or low jitter requirements. Select stability based on application: ±25ppm for general, ±10ppm for precision. Consider programmable oscillators for prototyping or custom frequencies.

faqs.question: When should I use an oscillator instead of a crystal?
faqs.answer: Use an oscillator when you need guaranteed start-up, don't want to design oscillator circuitry, need multiple clock outputs, or require features like output enable. Oscillators cost more but simplify design and reduce risk.

faqs.question: What is the difference between CMOS and differential output?
faqs.answer: CMOS is single-ended (0V to VDD) and suitable for most applications up to ~50MHz. Differential (LVDS/LVPECL) uses two complementary signals and is required for high-speed (>100MHz), low jitter, or noise-sensitive applications.

products.name: 50MHz Crystal Oscillator 3.2×2.5mm
products.shortDescription: High-performance 50MHz CMOS oscillator with ±25ppm stability and tri-state output enable. Ideal for networking and embedded processor clocking.
products.description: High-performance 50MHz CMOS output crystal oscillator with excellent frequency stability and low jitter, ideal for networking and communication applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The YXO-3225-50M is a high-performance 50MHz crystal oscillator featuring CMOS output and integrated oscillator circuitry. This active oscillator eliminates the need for external load capacitors and provides a clean, stable clock output ready to drive your digital circuits.

With ±25ppm frequency stability across the operating temperature range and low jitter of 1ps RMS typical, this oscillator is well-suited for networking equipment, FPGA clocking, and processor timing applications. The tri-state output enable feature allows power management through clock gating.

The 3.2×2.5mm package is industry-standard and compatible with most crystal resonator footprints, making it an easy upgrade path when active oscillator performance is needed. The 3.3V operation is compatible with most modern digital logic families.

alternativeParts.description: 5.0×3.2mm package version with same electrical specs

alternativeParts.tradeOff: Smaller size but slightly higher power consumption

faeReview.summary: This is an excellent general-purpose oscillator that I recommend frequently for networking and embedded applications. The tri-state enable feature is particularly useful for power management - you can disable the clock to put peripherals in low-power mode. The startup time of 5ms max is fast enough for most applications. I've used this in Ethernet switch designs, FPGA development boards, and industrial control systems with consistent reliability. The CMOS output drives standard logic levels well, and the 5ns rise/fall time is suitable for most digital applications. For cost-sensitive designs where you don't need the absolute lowest jitter, this oscillator delivers excellent value.
faeReview.author: Senior FAE Team

faqs.question: How do I use the Output Enable (OE) pin?
faqs.answer: Connect OE to VDD (or leave floating with internal pull-up) for normal operation. Connect to GND to disable output (tri-state/high-impedance). This allows clock gating for power management.

faqs.question: Can this oscillator drive multiple loads?
faqs.answer: The CMOS output can typically drive 1-2 standard CMOS loads. For multiple loads, use a clock buffer or ensure total load capacitance is under 15pF. Check the datasheet for specific drive capability.

faqs.question: What is the difference between crystal and oscillator jitter?
faqs.answer: Crystals have very low intrinsic jitter but the total system jitter depends on your oscillator circuit design. Active oscillators have specified jitter that includes all circuitry - this YXO-3225-50M has 1ps RMS typical jitter.

faqs.question: Can I use this with 1.8V or 2.5V logic?
faqs.answer: This is a 3.3V oscillator. For 1.8V or 2.5V systems, use a level shifter or choose the YXO-P programmable version which supports multiple voltages. Do not overdrive lower voltage logic with 3.3V output.

faqs.question: What happens if I exceed the maximum supply voltage?
faqs.answer: Exceeding 3.6V may damage the oscillator. Use a 3.3V regulator with good transient response. If your system has 5V available, regulate it down to 3.3V for the oscillator.

products.name: 156.25MHz Differential Oscillator LVDS
products.shortDescription: Ultra-low jitter differential oscillator with LVDS output for 10G Ethernet and high-speed communications. Sub-0.3ps RMS jitter performance.
products.description: High-performance 156.25MHz differential oscillator with LVDS output, designed for high-speed networking, fiber channel, and 10G Ethernet applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The YXO-D-156M25 is a high-performance differential oscillator designed for demanding high-speed applications. With 156.25MHz output and LVDS signaling, this oscillator is ideal for 10G Ethernet, Fiber Channel, and high-speed SerDes clocking applications.

The standout feature is the ultra-low jitter performance of less than 0.3ps RMS and excellent phase noise of -145dBc/Hz at 1MHz offset. These specifications ensure reliable operation of high-speed data converters and minimize bit error rates in communication systems.

The differential LVDS output provides excellent noise immunity and reduced EMI compared to single-ended signals. The integrated LDO regulator provides excellent power supply rejection, maintaining performance even with noisy system power rails.

alternativeParts.description: HCSL output version for PCIe applications
alternativeParts.tradeOff: Different output type, same frequency and jitter specs
Lower frequency for Gigabit Ethernet instead of 10G

faeReview.summary: This is an outstanding differential oscillator that rivals premium brands at a fraction of the cost. The sub-0.3ps jitter and excellent phase noise make it perfect for 10G+ applications. I've used this in 5G base station designs, high-speed data acquisition systems, and 10G Ethernet switches with excellent results. The LVDS output provides clean edges and excellent noise immunity. Pay attention to proper termination - use 100Ω differential termination at the receiver. Keep traces short and matched (<5mil length difference). The integrated LDO provides good PSRR, but I still recommend a ferrite bead and local decoupling for best performance. Highly recommended for any high-speed application.
faeReview.author: Senior FAE Team

faqs.question: How do I terminate the LVDS output?
faqs.answer: Place a 100Ω differential termination resistor across OUT+ and OUT- at the receiver end. Keep traces short (<2 inches) and matched in length. Use proper differential routing with consistent spacing.

faqs.question: What is phase noise and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: Phase noise measures frequency stability in the frequency domain. Lower phase noise (more negative dBc/Hz) means cleaner spectral purity. It's critical for high-speed communications as it affects bit error rates and system EVM.

faqs.question: Can I use this for PCIe clocking?
faqs.answer: PCIe requires HCSL output, not LVDS. Use the YXO-D-156M25-HCSL version for PCIe applications. The electrical specs are similar but the output driver is compatible with PCIe requirements.

faqs.question: How do I measure jitter accurately?
faqs.answer: Use a high-bandwidth oscilloscope (>1GHz) with jitter analysis software or a dedicated phase noise analyzer. For RMS jitter, integrate phase noise from 12kHz to 20MHz offset. Ensure proper probing to avoid measurement errors.

faqs.question: What power supply filtering is recommended?
faqs.answer: Use a ferrite bead in series with VDD followed by a 10μF and 0.1μF capacitor to ground. Place the 0.1μF capacitor as close to the oscillator as possible. This filtering maintains performance with noisy supplies.
